title: Babylon Loading
productionNotes: Open with deep sub-bass drone (40Hz) and glitchy digital artifacts—WiFi connection sounds, notification pings processed through heavy reverb. Nyabinghi heartbeat drum enters at 0:20, pitched down and bitcrushed. Sparse dub delays create spatial disorientation. Layer synthetic string pads with analog warmth fighting digital distortion. Keep vocals dry and intimate in verses, then drown in reverb/delay on chorus to symbolize losing oneself. No traditional drops—maintain hypnotic tension throughout with evolving texture rather than dynamics.
intro:
[Glitching sounds, WiFi connecting, notification chimes]
[Deep sub-bass hum, distorted Nyabinghi heartbeat]
[Whispered] Babylon loading... loading... loading...
verse1:
Eye dem open but mi spirit sleep
Thumb a scroll through di endless deep
Blue light baptism, three inna mornin'
Soul get hijacked without no warnin'
Algorithm know mi better than mi know miself
Dem sell mi back mi own reflection pon dem shelf
Double-tap fi salvation, heart react fi love
But Jah voice cyaan reach through di cloud above
prechorus:
Di buffering neva stop
Di buffering neva stop
Swipe right, swipe left
Nothing real left
chorus:
Babylon loading, loading, loading
Mi consciousness eroding, eroding, eroding
Thirty-three percent, mi soul corroding
Babylon loading, Jah... Babylon loading
verse2:
Dem give we filter fi hide we face
Dem give we follower fi fill di space
Where ancestor wisdom used to dwell
Now a just notification bell
Scroll past suffering, scroll past truth
Dem monetize di anguish of di youth
Virtual chains more heavy than steel
Cyaan even member what is real
bridge:
[Vocal chant, heavily processed]
Buffering... buffering... buffering...
Where is I? Where is I?
Lost inna di Wi-Fi
Lost inna di Wi-Fi
[Whispered] Connection unstable...
outro:
[All instruments strip away except sub-bass and heartbeat]
Babylon loading... ninety-nine percent...
[Glitch, distortion peak]
[Sudden silence]
[Single heartbeat]
promptForSuno: Deep dub reggae trap fusion, 68 BPM, E minor, Jamaican male baritone vocal with authentic patois, hypnotic Nyabinghi heartbeat drum pitched down and bitcrushed, heavy sub-bass drone, glitchy digital artifacts, sparse dub delays, synthetic pads with analog warmth, intimate dry verses, reverb-drenched chorus, ominous atmosphere, digital anxiety, spiritual numbness, dark ambient textures, no drops just evolving tension, WiFi sounds, notification pings processed, mystical Rastafarian consciousness, dystopian soundscape
